mnemonics.title: Key Capacity Metrics
mnemonics.content: C-M-D-N: CPU, Memory, Disk, Network - The four pillars of capacity tracking metrics that must always be monitored.

mnemonics.title: Threshold Alerting Process
mnemonics.content: S-T-A-R: Set Threshold, Trigger Alert, Action Required - Remember the alerting workflow.

mnemonics.title: Agent vs Agentless
mnemonics.content: A-B-A: Agent-Based = Deep Analysis; Agentless = Broad Coverage - Choose based on monitoring needs.

acronyms.usage: Standard protocol for network device monitoring and data collection in capacity tracking tools.
Windows-based technology used by agentless tools to collect system performance data.
Critical metrics like CPU % and memory usage that define system health in capacity tracking.
Enables capacity tracking tools to integrate with other IT management systems.
Refers to data transfer between systems and storage devices, measured in capacity tracking.

visualTips.visualization: Think of a car's dashboard: speedometer shows real-time speed, while trip computer shows historical fuel efficiency trends.
Like a thermometer with a fever line - when temperature crosses the line (threshold), alarm sounds (alert).
Agent-based is like having a dedicated reporter in each office; agentless is like a supervisor walking around observing without staying.
Imagine a glass filled with water - the water level represents utilization percentage, the empty space is available capacity.

analogies.analogy: Like a person's normal body temperature - you know what's normal for them, and any deviation indicates potential illness.
Like a fitness tracker for your IT infrastructure - continuously monitoring vital signs to ensure healthy operation.
Like a smoke detector - set at a specific smoke level, it alerts you when danger threshold is crossed.
Like summarizing a month's weather into daily averages - making complex data manageable and meaningful.
